title: Causal Latent Tyre State Features
summary: A 2026 F1 tyre degradation paper models tyre pace as a latent stochastic state with pit stops as resets.
rationale: A 2026 F1 tyre degradation paper models tyre pace as a latent stochastic state with pit stops as resets. The current features use same-row ratios and group medians, but not a causal evolving state estimate within each driver-race-stint.
implementation_hint: Sort by Year/Race/Driver/LapNumber and compute only past-looking EWM/rolling residuals: lap_time minus race-lap median, EWM residual slope, residual acceleration, rolling variance, and post-pit reset age. Use shift(1) before rolling to avoid same-lap leakage.
expected_effect: Should capture degradation cliffs and noisy tyre condition better than static TyreLife or Cumulative_Degradation features, potentially improving AUC on borderline pit-window laps.
risk: Requires row order to be trustworthy; non-causal rolling or train-test concatenation without careful shifting can create validation leakage.
